name: Leadfeeder
description:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

What Leadfeeder Does

Leadfeeder identifies companies visiting your website (even without form fills) and enriches visits with company data and behavior signals. It’s focused on turning anonymous traffic into warm leads.

Leadfeeder is best for agencies that run lead magnets, SEO/ads, and want to capture mid-funnel intent signals.

However, Leadfeeder only helps if you have meaningful website traffic. If you’re primarily doing outbound prospecting with no inbound strategy, this tool won’t surface any leads.

Source: Leadfeeder

Key Features

  • Company visitor identification from web traffic.
  • Daily lead feeds and visit detail pages.
  • Integration with CRMs and marketing workflows (Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, Slack, Google Ads).
  • Firmographic enrichment for identified companies
  • Alerts for target account activity
  • Filtering to remove bots, ISPs, and low-quality traffic

Benefits of Leadfeeder

  • Capture mid-funnel intent (visitors who researched but didn’t convert).
  • Prioritize warm leads who already visited your site.
  • Marketing attribution clarity on which campaigns, content, and channels drive high-value visitor traffic
  • Enable faster follow-up on high-intent accounts

Pricing

Leadfeeder offers a free forever plan and usage-based paid plans: Paid plans start at €141/month. A discount is available when choosing to bill annually.

Pros

✅Great for surfacing warm prospects and campaign attribution.

✅Easy setup with Google Analytics

✅Strong visibility into buyer behavior and content engagement

Cons

🚫Not a cold prospect database; must have decent inbound traffic to be useful.

🚫Company-level identification only (no guaranteed individual contacts)

🚫Identification rates vary significantly

‍
what_people_say:
__POSSIBLE_UNTRANSLATED__

What Customers Say

“I can view website visits and see exactly what they were looking at on website.

The only thing I wish was different is that it only says a person viewed your website from xyz company, not actually who it was.”

- Review collected by and hosted on G2.com.
